name: Synthetic CASE Essays Support AES Training While Real Essays Carry Validation and Test Evidence
statement: Yoo et al. use 40,185 synthetic DREsS_CASE essays for rubric-based AES training augmentation while preserving real classroom DREsS_New essays for validation and test splits, making synthetic training acceptable only within a real-response evaluation boundary.
population_or_context: Rubric-based EFL automated essay scoring dataset and baselines.
quantitative_note: The dataset includes 2,279 real classroom essays, 6,515 standardized existing-dataset samples, and 40,185 synthetic CASE samples.
caveats: CASE corruption is not the same as LLM-generated student simulation.
The evidence supports training augmentation, not synthetic-only validation.
Rubric-based EFL essay scoring may not generalize to short-answer or SJT scoring.
evaluation_method: Automated essay scoring evaluation with real validation/test data and rubric-score agreement metrics.
reported_metrics: Dataset sizes and weighted-kappa-style AES evaluation metrics.
limitations: Does not validate a scorer using only synthetic responses.
Supports a training/augmentation use case with real-response validation controls.

source_locator.quote_or_paraphrase_note: The finding paraphrases the DREsS_New, DREsS_Std, and DREsS_CASE data split and validation/test boundary.
